title: ZeroAvia to Build Manufacturing Hub in Scotland

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Zero-emission aviation leader will launch a Hydrogen Centre of Excellence and major manufacturing facility close to Glasgow Airport

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: ZeroAvia to Build Manufacturing Hub in Scotland | ZeroAvia News Scottish Enterprise have awarded ZeroAvia a £9m Regional Selective Assistance grant to help realise the project alongside company investment, creating hundreds of jobs

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The manufacturing plans build on UK Government R&D funding and equity investment into ZeroAvia Scotland’s First Minister John Swinney and UK Government Secretary of State for Scotland Ian Murray launches the new project alongside ZeroAvia CEO Val Miftakhov

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – ZeroAvia to Build Manufacturing Hub in Scotland
